title: Field Lines, Repetition, Combination, Structured Fields, and Limits · Program Defense
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for field lines, repetition, combination, structured fields, and limits and requires the smallest strict loss-aware bounded field-section model with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for loss-aware bounded field-section model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse case-insensitive token field names, colon delimiters, optional field whitespace, and field values while rejecting whitespace before the colon.
Preserve field-line order and repeated values, combining only when that field definition permits it.
Parse Connection options and nominate hop-by-hop fields dynamically instead of relying only on a fixed list.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Field Name Value Grammar · changed case · Quiz Field Name Value Grammar
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz protocol team handles case 9266 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve repeated field lines and reject sections beyond a bounded policy; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
